title: Decoding The Epitranscriptome: In Silico Insights Into m6A Regulatory Network In Breast Cancer.
snippet: N6-methyladenosine (m6A) is the most abundant internal RNA modification in eukaryotic transcripts and plays a critical role in RNA metabolism, gene expression, and cellular homeostasis. Dysregulation of m6A regulators, including "writers," "erasers," and "readers", has been increasingly implicated in cancer biology; however, their comprehensive roles in bre...
